column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind
column13: OPEN LEADS DOWNSTREAM THROUGH VARIOUS SECTIONS OF THE RIVER IN BETWEEN THE TWO LAKES. ICE HAS SUNKEN IN AREAS WHERE FLOW IS
column14: @ Uncertainty: ISO method, 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @  HAD TO DO MEASUREMENT UPSTREAM OF WHERE OPEN WATER MEASUREMENTS ARE COMPLETED AND MORE SLIGHTLY INTO THE LAKE BECAUSE ICE HAS FROZEN TO BOTTOM ON THE SHALLOW EDGE AREAS CREATING A DEFINED CHANNEL VISIBLE WITH SUNKEN ICE INDICATING WHERE THE FLOW WAS. INTIAL ICE TESTS FURTHER DOWNSTREAM SHOWED WEAK ICE BUT WE WERE ABLE TO FIND A TRANSECT WITH SUFFICIENT ICE IN THE FLOW CHANNEL. OFFSET BEFORE 1.91 AND AFTER 1.391. COMPLETED DWL BEFORE MEASUREMENT AND OBTAINED DWL AFTER BASED ON CUT. Mid-section
HG: 6.559 Reset (RS)
HG: 4.997 Reset (RS)

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: COMPLETE ICE COVER, A FEW OPEN LEADS DOWNSTREAM OF THE OUTLET THROUGH THE CHANNEL VISIBLE WHEN FLYING OVER
column14: @ Uncertainty: ISO method, 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ ICE COVER FROM BANK TO BANK BUT FLOW ONLY FOUND IN THE CENTER CHANNEL AS EXPECTED FROM PREVIOUS MEASUREMENTS. PUT NEW OFFSETS IN AT 1.91. NITROGEN LINE FROZEN. TANK CHANGE COMPLETED. Mid-section
HG: 6.202 Surge: 0.020  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  Reset (RS)

@ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ SLOW MOVING FLOW PLUS WIND BLOWING UPSTREAM MADE FOR DIFFICULT TRANSECTS. LARGE BOULDERS JUST BELOW SURFACE ON RIVER RIGHT, ATTEMPTED PAIR OF TRANSECTS INTO BOULDERS ON RIVER RIGHT AS FAR AS BOAT COULD GO AND THEN ANOTHER PAIR WITH LARGE ESTIMATE TAKEN JUST AFTER THE DEEP CHANNEL. ISSUES WITH MOVING BED TEST ASSOCIATED WITH SAME ISSUES AS TRANSECTS. OFFSET BEFORE RESET 1.392 AND AFTER 1.396 ADCP by Moving Boat
HG: 6.204 Surge: 0.010  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.
HG: 6.293 Surge: 0.000  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  Reset (RS)

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13: OPEN LEADS IN RIVER DOWNSTREAM OF THE GAUGE IN THE CHANNEL BETWEEN 2 LAKE AREAS.
column14: ATTEMPTED MEASUREMENT AT A SECTION OF THE RIVER BETWEEN THE TWO LAKE AREAS THAT CONTAINED AN OPEN LEAD. STREAM PRO BOAT TOO SMALL FOR CONDITIONS AS CONSTANT SUBMARINING WAS AN ISSUE. LARGE AMOUNT OF EDGE ICE PARTICULARLY ON RIVER LEFT. NO LAMINER FLOW PRESENT IN LOCATIONS ABLE TO MEASURE. MGH OBTAINED FROM AQUARIUS POST FIELD. @ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ OFFSET BEFORE 1.401 AND AFTER RESET 1.388 ADCP by Moving Boat

HG: 6.268 Surge: 0.002  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind
column14: ICE COVER ON MAJORITY OF THE MAIN BODY OF THE LAKE BUT ONLY MINOR ICE FLOW IN THE RIVER. UNABLE TO BOAT FROM GAUGE TO MEASUREMENT SECTION SO ZODIAC WITH RIVER PRO WAS USED.  Corrected M.G.H. includes Sensor Reset Correction of 0.002 and Gage Correction of 0.000 applied to Weighted M.G.H of 7.295 MAJORITY OF THE LAKE STILL ICED OVER, EDGES MAINLY MELTED. TWO BM'S UNDER WATER.  ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : ; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Upstream
column14: Frontsheet notes: 1. SxS used to incorporate Backwater Conditions 2. CIC at gauge and outlet (regular mmt section) 3. visit with helicopter to attempt open water tethered boat mmt at mostly open narrow section 2 km ds of gauge. Some hanging ice/edge ice. Hinged/hanging ice broken away and SxS used to incorporate edge ice. measurement only due to time constraints from helicopter travel time.  SxS measurement notes: 3.000 m: Panel was originally normal.  Post processed for ice cover of estimated thickness.  original bottom track depth was1.037 m.  Depth  changed to 0.737 to accomodate the estimated 0.3m ws to bottom of ice 14.500 m: Panel was originally normal.  Post processed for ice cover of estimated thickness.  original bottom track depth was1.198 m.  Depth  changed to 0.898 to accomodate the estimated 0.3m ws to bottom of ice
